output.final_deliverable:
1. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Science
3. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Physics
4. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gravity
Adjacency pair: Wikipedia's 'Science' page links to 'Physics' (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Physics), confirming that 'Science' links to 'Physics'.
output.action_summary: The chain starts from Wikipedia's Main Page, then goes to the Science page, which links to the Physics page, and then to the Gravity page. An adjacency pair is verified by checking the links on the Science page.

94cfc14f-f2d5-4fcf-b8c6-f1fc0ec12750.title:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

73390bc3-6295-48dc-866f-414c1b385dd7.title: Build a chain of Wikipedia URLs starting from Main Page

details.step 1: Search for the Wikipedia Main Page to identify initial links. Use a search query if needed, but mainly visit https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page.
details.planning: Visit the page to obtain the full list of linked Wikipedia URLs on the main page. This is the starting point for constructing the chain.
verification_steps: Verify that the URLs are valid Wikipedia URLs and are present on the page.
Ensure the links are clickable and are direct links from the main page content.
measurable_outputs: List of initial Wikipedia URLs linked from Main Page
details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Establishes the confirmed starting point and ensures that subsequent steps can reliably follow listed links.
details.action: Sequential search and visit strategy to construct the URL chain with verification of link adjacency
details.parent_goal:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

056281af-3062-4a26-966c-965c7cf0a9e7.title: Select the second URL in the chain from the links on Main Page

details.step 2: From the list of links obtained from the Main Page, select one Wikipedia URL to visit next.
details.planning: Visit the selected URL, extract all links, and identify candidate URLs for the third step.
target_facts: Links from the second Wikipedia page
verification_steps: Check URL validity, verify that links are part of the Wikipedia domain, and confirm the links are from the page content.
measurable_outputs: List of Wikipedia URLs linked from the second page
details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Ensures the chain continues with verified links and builds evidence for the adjacency between pages.
details.action: Visit and extract links from the second Wikipedia page in the chain
details.parent_goal:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

e43818cd-6932-4824-b968-c8d574a00ac0.title: Choose the third Wikipedia URL based on links from the second page

details.step 3: Similarly, select a link from the second page's links to progress to the third URL.
details.planning: Visit the third URL, extract its links, and select the next URL to continue the chain.
target_facts: Links from the third Wikipedia page
verification_steps: Verify the URL's source and validate links
details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Further confirmation of link continuity and supports chaining rather than arbitrary link selection.
details.action: Visit the third Wikipedia URL and extract its links to continue the chain
details.parent_goal:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

ab1a54c0-150b-4dda-ba1b-330d5f28e1da.title: Identify a link from the third page to the fourth page and verify adjacency

details.step 4: Select a link on the third page which points to the intended fourth URL, visit this page, and verify the link's existence.
details.planning: Use link extraction to confirm that this URL appears on the third page and that visiting it confirms the adjacency.
target_facts: Existence of link from third to fourth URL
verification_steps: Check the presence of the link on the third page before visiting, then verify the link is clickable and leads to the correct page.
measurable_outputs: Confirmed URL chain with verified adjacency
details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Provides concrete evidence that the chain is correctly linked from the third to the fourth page, satisfying the task's requirement.
details.action: Visit the fourth Wikipedia URL from links on the third page and verify the link exists
